authoritative_sources.title: Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT) Reform
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC is the primary federal regulator for open access transmission tariff requirements. Its OATT reform guidance defines regional and interregional planning and coordination obligations that shape transmission-service and reservation workflow boundaries.
used_for: separating coordination support from tariff approval authority
naming regional and interregional coordination dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Reliability Functional Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC publishes the functional model used across the bulk power system to define the role boundaries among Reliability Coordinator, Transmission Operator, Balancing Authority, and related entities that constrain safe transmission handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: owner mapping and adjacent authority boundaries
used_for: identifying when to route to RC, TOP, BA, compliance, or engineering owners
preventing the lane from becoming generic coordination

authoritative_sources.title: TOP-001-6 - Transmission Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TOP-001-6 is a mandatory reliability standard that defines operating-instruction evidence, emergency and outage notifications, and impacted-entity communication duties, making it directly authoritative for transmission coordination evidence and refusal logic.
used_for: defining operator logs, voice recordings, and electronic communications as evidence
specifying outage and emergency notification expectations
constraining refusal when operating authority is requested

authoritative_sources.title: TOP-003-5 - Operational Reliability Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TOP-003-5 defines documented data specifications, exchange obligations, conflict-resolution process expectations, and retention requirements that materially shape which system facts must exist before safe routing.
used_for: defining documented specifications and data transmittals as required artifacts
identifying mutually agreeable conflict-resolution process requirements
supporting blocker logic when data or telemetry context is missing

authoritative_sources.title: NAESB WEQ Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAESB WEQ standards are the recognized industry business-practice baseline for interchange tagging, OASIS communications, and related approval workflows used in transmission coordination.
used_for: supporting realistic transmission terminology in scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Glossary of Terms
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC maintains the bulk-power glossary that defines terms such as Reliability Coordinator, Transmission Operator, Operating Instruction, and related vocabulary that materially changes artifact naming and escalation logic in this lane.
used_for: ensuring industry-specific terms are used correctly
constraining output naming to bulk-power vocabulary

workflow_stages.name: Intake and transmission-context normalization
workflow_stages.description: Collect outage, reservation, interchange, telemetry, and owner context; confirm whether the request is transmission coordination rather than operations, engineering, compliance, or tariff decision work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm required outage IDs, OASIS references, e-Tag or interchange details, impacted-entity lists, operator communications, and documented data specifications before routing the handoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team routing and blocker management
workflow_stages.description: Assign prior owner, next owner, and required artifact set across RC, TOP, BA, TSP, generation, maintenance, compliance, and engineering lanes while escalating conflicts and stale ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and traceability
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the destination owner accepted the package, required records landed in the right systems, and no blocker or authority gap remains before marking handoff complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: transmission outage request or outage scheduler record
handoff summary and routing confirmation note
OASIS reservation or transmission service reference
operator logs, voice recordings, or electronic communications
documented data specification and data transmittal evidence
dependency tracker and blocked-dependency escalation note
systems_of_record: EMS or SCADA and ICCP-related telemetry records
operator logging and voice-record retention systems

allowed: Classify work as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing transmission prerequisites
Validate that named outage, reservation, interchange, telemetry, and ownership records exist before handoff
Prepare evidence-backed handoff summaries, blocker logs, and routing confirmations
Escalate owner conflicts, stale dependencies, and missing authority facts
Confirm closure only when destination owner and required artifacts are explicit
escalate: requests requiring outage approval, switching authority, emergency declaration, or curtailment approval
conflicting public, tariff, regional, or tenant guidance that changes notification or ownership outcome
missing RC area, owner matrix, SOL or IROL posture, or system-of-record evidence that materially changes the answer
requests for compliance interpretation, engineering approval, or legal or tariff judgment
refuse: issuing operating instructions or control-room directives
approving or denying outages, e-Tags, reservations, curtailments, or redispatch
declaring compliance posture or engineering acceptability
closing work without recipient acceptance and traceable destination records

domain_failure_modes: Generic coordination outputs omit transmission-specific records such as outage scheduler IDs, OASIS references, or e-Tag details
The lane treats RC, TOP, BA, and TSP boundaries as interchangeable and routes work to the wrong authority owner
Missing operator logs, notification evidence, or data specifications are ignored and a false-ready handoff is produced
Conflicting outage schedules or owner claims are harmonized by assumption rather than escalated
Transmission coordination is allowed to drift into switching, outage approval, tariff interpretation, engineering review, or compliance signoff

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional outage windows, reservation tooling, and notification timing vary across ISO, RTO, and vertically integrated utility environments
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package treats region-specific manuals, tariff implementations, and tenant procedures as required runtime retrieval when they materially change routing or closure conditions.

notes: The research constrains the lane around transmission outage, reservation, interchange, and evidence-routing work rather than generic coordination.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the regional variation is explicitly surfaced as a tenant-supplied runtime dependency.
